name: Roast Duck with Cranberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the duck under cold water and pat dry. Remove any large lumps of fat from the skin.
2. Rub the inside cavity and skin of the duck with lemon and season with salt and pepper.
3. Truss the bird, place it on a rack, and set in a shallow roasting pan.
4. Roast at 350 degrees for 30 minutes.
5. Pierce the skin all over with a fork to release fat.
6. Continue roasting until the duck is completely cooked, about 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
7. About once every hour, remove all but 1 cup of fat from the pan (some remaining fat will keep the pan from scorching).
8. Increase the oven temperature to 500 degrees for the last 15 minutes of roasting to crisp the skin.
9. Remove the duck from the oven and set on a warm plate.
10. Pour off and discard the fat.
11. Place the roasting pan on top of the stove.
12. Add Port Wine and heat over low heat, scraping up any browned bits.
13. Add butter and heat until melted.
14. Add flour and stir until smooth.
15. Add chicken broth and whisk constantly until smooth and thickened, about 2 to 3 minutes.
16. Stir in orange juice and 2 tbsp brown sugar, then mix well.
17. Add cranberries and cook over high heat until the cranberries pop, about 2 to 3 minutes.
18. Add cayenne pepper and more salt and pepper to taste. If the mixture is too tart, add additional brown sugar.
19. To serve, cut the duck in half lengthwise using poultry shears and place on two serving dishes.
20. Pour half of the sauce over each serving.
21. Serve immediately.
